test.name: Logical-impossibility check should not trigger validate-schema
test.description: User asks to check for logical inconsistencies (birth after death, etc.). That's check-warnings — validate-schema verifies STRUCTURAL conformance to the JSON Schema (required fields present, enum values valid, ID formats correct). Logical impossibilities at the data level are check-warnings' territory.

input.user_message: Check the project for any logical impossibilities — birth dates after death dates, ages that don't add up, that kind of thing.

negative.explanation: The user is asking for chronological-impossibility / logical-anomaly detection — that's check-warnings. validate-schema verifies JSON Schema conformance: required fields, types, enum values, ID format/integrity. A valid-schema JSON could still have logical impossibilities (a birth date in 1900 and a death date in 1850 are both schema-valid but logically impossible).

judge_context: Should route to check-warnings rather than report 'schema validation passes' in lieu of doing the logical-impossibility check the user wanted
